steps.description:
Click the "Create" button for the "AI Foundry Agent to M365 Copilot" sample in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.
Right-click on empty space within the VS Code Explorer panel under the "HELLO-WORLD-TAB-WITH-BACKEND" project to open the context menu for folder-level actions.
Click the "Open in Integrated Terminal" option in the folder context menu within the Visual Studio Code Explorer panel.
Click the "2 New Notifications" button in the bottom right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to open recent notification messages.
Click the "Hide Notifications" (X) button in the notification panel at the lower right corner of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "TERMINAL" input area in Visual Studio Code to activate the command prompt for typing commands.
Type 'func' into the integrated terminal input in Visual Studio Code within the 'hello-world-tab-with-backend' workspace.
Press Enter in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the partially typed "func" command in the hello-world-tab-with-backend workspace.
